'''Olly Russell''' (born 21 September 1998) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} & {{rlp|sh}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

In 2019 he spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at the [[Leigh Centurions]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], as well as [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

==Background==
Russell was born in [[Oldham, England]].

Russell played his junior amateur rugby for Oldham Saint Anne's, spending some time at Wigan St Judes.

He is a product of the Giants Academy after a spell with Wigan's Academy.

==Career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
After a spell with Wigan, he moved to Huddersfield's academy side and was an integral part of their run to the academy semi final in 2018.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://wiganwarriors.com/news/2018-09-22-under-19s-cruise-to-grand-final |title=Under-19s cruise to Grand Final {{!}} News {{!}} Wigan Warriors |website=wiganwarriors.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190527152113/https://wiganwarriors.com/news/2018-09-22-under-19s-cruise-to-grand-final |archive-date=2019-05-27}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://saddind.co.uk/olly-delighted-to-play-the-generation-game/|title = Olly delighted to play the generation game|date = 8 June 2018}}</ref>
In 2018 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] against the [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43966316|title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants 28-18 Widnes Vikings|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Russell played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers (loan)===
On 26 May 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=26 May 2021|accessdate=27 May 2021|title=Huddersfield Giants sextet set for Championship loan moves|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-loan-championship-rugby-20683895.amp}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 28 June 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] on a four-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Wakefield Trinity|date=28 Jun 2024|accessdate=29 Sep 2024|title=Russell Becomes The Newest Trinity Player|url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/russell-becomes-the-newest-trinity-player/}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils (loan)===
On 9 July 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Jul 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Wakefield Trinity half-back leaves club in deal to join Super League rivals|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-half-back-leaves-club-in-deal-to-join-super-league-rivals}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (loan)===
On 20 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on loan for the remainder of the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=20 Aug 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Huddersfield Giants bring back former halfback on loan|url=https://www.totalrl.com/huddersfield-giants-bring-back-former-halfback-on-loan/}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (re-join)===
On 18 November 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 Nov 2025|accessdate=19 Nov 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Wakefield Trinity half-back departs for Super League rivals in pre-season transfer deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-trinity-olly-russell-signs-huddersfield-giants}}</ref>
